steps.title: Rearrange and factor to find the solutions
steps.content:
Start by moving all terms to the left side:

$$(x-k)^{2}-(k-4a)(x-k) = 0$$

Factor out $(x-k)$:

$$(x-k)\left[(x-k)-(k-4a)\right] = 0$$

Simplify the brackets:

$$(x-k)(x - 2k + 4a) = 0$$

This gives two solutions: $x = k$ or $x = 2k - 4a$.

steps.title: Apply the sum condition to find a
steps.content:
The sum of the two solutions is:

$$k + (2k - 4a) = 3k - 4a$$

We're given this sum equals $3k + 35$:

$$3k - 4a = 3k + 35$$

Subtract $3k$ from both sides:

$$-4a = 35$$

Divide by $-4$:

$$a = -\frac{35}{4}$$

Equivalent forms accepted: -35/4, -8.75.
